policy.name: ROUTE_WEEK_RETROACTIVE_REPLACEMENT_V1
policy.meaning: The route file is a complete authoritative snapshot for the whole effective week. There is no intra-week validity period.

repo_static_evidence: scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py uses public.ruta_rutero ON CONFLICT (source, source_row).
scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py records source_row from IdRow when present, otherwise row index + 2.
scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py infers effective_week_start from loaded_at in America/Santiago.
scripts/cg_canonical_build_local.py uses an explicit route_source_plan with week_start and source_ruta_batch_ids.
scripts/refresh_control_gestion_v2_incremental.py uses cg_core.v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2 and carries both visitas_exigidas_semana and day plan fields.
app/db.py uses public.ruta_rutero through RUTA_TABLE for inventory/client selector flows and uses Control Gestion v2 mart/view surfaces for CG.

answers.more_than_one_physical_row_may_be_legiti: Yes, but only as input-level operational detail. The resolved weekly compliance surface must collapse deterministically to one logical route-frequency record per effective_week_start + COD_RT + CLIENTE unless Bastian explicitly approves multirow semantics.
answers.exact_duplicates_can_be_excluded_without: Technically yes for current/resolved surfaces because all business fields match, but Bastian should approve whether exact duplicate rows remain in immutable history as source evidence.
answers.stable_physical_key_needed: A stable physical key is not required for replacement semantics. Immutable batch_id + source_row is enough for audit. Current replacement must be snapshot-scoped, not source_row-upsert-scoped.

current_mart_usage.weekly_count_authority: cg_core.v_rr_frecuencia_base_resuelta_v2.visitas_exigidas_semana, sourced from RUTA_RUTERO VECES POR SEMANA
current_mart_usage.day_plan_authority: LUNES..DOMINGO plan fields are carried as LUNES_PLAN..DOMINGO_PLAN and compared with daily flags in mart exports/checklists

canonical_builder_usage.day_plan_authority: route_week_snapshot lunes..domingo, surfaced as *_PLAN in canonical outputs

frequency_analysis.reason_not_blocking: The current mart and canonical builder already preserve both fields. The mismatch is business semantics, not a loader safety failure, as long as it is surfaced and reviewed.

load_strategy_options.description: Keep source + source_row as current public upsert key.
load_strategy_options.consumer_impact: low code impact but unsafe data semantics
load_strategy_options.rationale: CG005A proved 26 stale source rows and 3342 source_row identity changes. This cannot represent full-week replacement.

load_strategy_options.description: Upsert current surface by a derived business key such as COD_RT + CLIENTE.
load_strategy_options.rationale: COD_RT + CLIENTE is the logical compliance grain, but the file has 34 duplicate groups. A business-key upsert would collapse or overwrite multirow input before Bastian decides semantics.

load_strategy_options.description: Treat each file as complete current source and replace all current public rows for source.
load_strategy_options.rationale: Fixes stale rows and reordering risk, but alone does not pin effective week or preserve immutable historical route lineage.

load_strategy_options.description: Stop maintaining public.ruta_rutero and resolve route exclusively from immutable batches.
load_strategy_options.rationale: Best historical lineage model, but app/db.py and cliente/inventory flows still consume public.ruta_rutero.

load_strategy_options.description: Immutable batch history plus full replacement of current public source plus explicit week-batch assignment.
load_strategy_options.rationale: Preserves public.ruta_rutero for existing consumers, keeps immutable batch evidence, removes source_row residue risk, and makes weekly route selection explicit.

selected_load_strategy.name: Batch historico inmutable + reemplazo completo de superficie vigente + asignacion explicita semana-batch
selected_load_strategy.current_surface_rule: Delete/replace the complete public.ruta_rutero source slice inside the same transaction after staging and before commit. Do not upsert by source_row.
selected_load_strategy.history_rule: Never delete immutable cg_core.ruta_rutero_load_rows or batch metadata as part of normal replacement.
selected_load_strategy.resolved_week_rule: Resolve Control Gestion through explicit effective_week_start to ruta_batch_id assignment rather than loaded_at inference.
selected_load_strategy.duplicate_rule: Preserve source rows in immutable history. For current/resolved surfaces, exact duplicate collapse and non-exact multirow aggregation need Bastian decision before implementation.

explicit_week_strategy_options.evaluation: Useful but too rigid by itself. It makes single-week loads simple, but does not model reassignments, rollback, or possible future multi-week reuse cleanly.
Selected. It records effective_week_start, route_batch_id, policy_version, assignment status, input hash, resolved surface hash, and previous assignment for rollback.
Reject as sole authority. It helps audit, but DB views and refreshes need DB-visible truth.
Reject. It can assign the wrong week if the load runs after the target week and cannot represent retroactive replacement policy.

explicit_week_strategy.current_view_impact: v_ruta_rutero_load_batch_week_v2 or a successor should prefer explicit assignments; loaded_at inference may remain only as legacy/backfill fallback with a visible source flag.
explicit_week_strategy.historical_compatibility: Existing 19 batches can remain inferable until explicitly assigned; no historical mutation is required for this design phase.

pseudoflow: Run source-check and block on missing critical columns.
Compute workbook SHA-256 and schema_signature before any DB action.
Normalize workbook into a temp/staging frame and classify exact/grain duplicates.
Require --effective-week-start 2026-06-08 and validate it is a Monday and matches the intended 2026-06-08..2026-06-14 week.
Require explicit confirmation token for ROUTE_WEEK_RETROACTIVE_REPLACEMENT_V1 before --apply.
Create route batch metadata in pending/cancelled-safe state with source, hash, schema signature, row counts, duplicate metrics, and effective_week_start in notes or new metadata.
Insert immutable history rows for the batch; keep source_row only as source-file physical audit position.
Validate inserted history row count, row_hash aggregate, source_row uniqueness, and accepted row count.
Replace current public.ruta_rutero source slice from the validated staging/history rows. This replacement deletes only the current source slice, never historical batches.
Write or activate effective_week_start to ruta_batch_id assignment with policy version ROUTE_WEEK_RETROACTIVE_REPLACEMENT_V1 and previous assignment reference.
Run resolved-view postcheck for the target week: rows present, no duplicate logical grains after resolver, expected batch visible, source hash/row count recorded.
Finalize batch status ok only after public replacement and week assignment checks pass.
Emit JSON with batch id, assignment id, row counts, hashes, postcheck status, rollback metadata, and observation draft.
rollback_conditions_before_commit: hash mismatch versus supplied expected hash
Bastian decision required but not provided
deletes: Only public.ruta_rutero rows for the specific source/current surface inside the replacement transaction.
never_deletes: mart facts or materialized views outside an explicitly authorized refresh phase
transaction_contract.postvalidation_failure: ROLLBACK if failure occurs before commit. If failure is detected after commit, execute a separate rollback command that reactivates previous week assignment and restores public.ruta_rutero from the previous batch or recorded backup snapshot.

rollback_contract.precommit: Single transaction rollback restores public.ruta_rutero, batch status, history inserts, and assignment changes.
postcommit: Read previous active assignment for effective_week_start.
Create rollback metadata row referencing failed assignment.
Replace public.ruta_rutero source slice from the previous approved batch/history snapshot, or from an explicit current-surface backup created in the apply transaction.
Mark failed assignment superseded/rolled_back; do not delete its immutable batch evidence.
Run the same resolved-view postcheck and emit rollback JSON.

proposed_cli.command_shape: python scripts/load_ruta_rutero_from_excel.py --excel data/DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx --sheet RUTA_RUTERO --source DB_GLOBAL_INVENTARIO.xlsx:RUTA_RUTERO --effective-week-start YYYY-MM-DD [--source-check-only|--dry-run|--apply] --confirm-weekly-replacement ROUTE_WEEK_RETROACTIVE_REPLACEMENT_V1 --json-out <path>

modes.--source-check-only: No DB write; validates workbook shape and source-check contract.
modes.--dry-run: Default; computes hashes, duplicate/frequency classification, planned deletes/inserts, and expected postchecks without writes.
modes.--apply: DB write path, not authorized by this contract; must be separately approved.

consumer_impact.consumer: app/db.py RUTA_TABLE / public.ruta_rutero selectors and inventory/client helpers
consumer_impact.impact: Requires no immediate runtime change if public.ruta_rutero remains current and fully replaced per source. Would require change under immutable-batch-only.
Materialized views must be refreshed only in an explicitly authorized phase; contract itself does not refresh.
The script can remain stable if view columns stay compatible, but the DB view must use explicit week-batch assignment instead of loaded_at inference.
Already models explicit week_start and source_ruta_batch_ids.
Can report current public/history and resolved week evidence. Future implementation may add explicit assignment metrics.
Must prefer explicit assignment for forward loads; loaded_at may remain legacy fallback.
Useful evidence for existing semantics; not runtime implementation authority.

bastian_decisions.decision: Whether the 31 non-exact COD_RT + CLIENTE duplicate groups are legitimate multiple operational rows or workbook defects.
bastian_decisions.technical_default: Treat them as MULTIPLE_OPERATIONAL_ROWS candidates, preserve in history, and block automatic logical collapse until approved.

bastian_decisions.decision: Whether the 3 exact duplicate rows may be excluded from current/resolved surfaces while still preserving them in immutable history.
bastian_decisions.technical_default: Safe to dedupe exact duplicates for current/resolved surfaces after approval.

bastian_decisions.decision: Whether VECES POR SEMANA or the sum of LUNES-DOMINGO is the business authority when they differ.
bastian_decisions.technical_default: Keep VECES POR SEMANA as weekly count authority and LUNES-DOMINGO as day plan authority, warning on mismatch.

bastian_decisions.decision: Whether the 70 bajas versus current surface are expected removals for week 2026-06-08.
bastian_decisions.technical_default: Treat bajas as expected full-snapshot removals only after Bastian approval.
